_comment: Executive Committee, newest term first. To add a term: copy a block, update label and members. 'img' is optional — people without a photo get the lotus logo placeholder.

members.name: Sharon A. Suh
members.card: Professor of Buddhism, Seattle University. Ph.D., Harvard.
members.bio: Sharon A. Suh is a professor of Buddhism in the Department of Theology and Religious Studies at Seattle University. She received her Ph.D. in Buddhist Studies from Harvard University and has published three books: Being Buddhist in a Christian World: Gender and Community; Silver Screen Buddha: Buddhism in Asian and Western Film; and Occupy This Body: A Buddhist Memoir.

members.name: Buphyun Sunim
members.bio: Buphyun Sunim was born in Korea and has completed 12 traditional Korean meditation retreats. She graduated from college in 1994, ordained as a novice nun in 2005, and received full ordination as a bhiksuni in 2012. Since 2019, she has been studying at the Graduate School for International Buddhist English at Dongguk University at Gyeongju.

members.name: Thich Nu Tien Lien
members.card: Bhiksuni, San Jose. Founder of multiple temples.
members.bio: Thich Nu Tien Lien was born in Saigon, Vietnam, in 1966. She enjoyed learning about Buddhism from a young age, received novice ordination in 1983, and became fully ordained as a bhiksuni in 1985. She earned a Master's degree in Social Work at San Diego State University and has founded several temples in San Jose, California.

members.name: Kaytee Sumida
members.card: Buddhist Temple San Diego liaison. 12 years on the EC.
members.bio: Kaytee Sumida was born and raised in Los Angeles, California. She became interested in Asian religions by reading folk tales in Chinese literature and culture classes. She received a B.S. in Medical Technology from California State University and an M.S. from San Francisco State University. She has been a member of the Buddhist Temple San Diego since 1990 and serves as liaison with the Interreligious Council of San Diego.

members.name: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo
members.card: Bhikṣuṇī, Drukpa Kagyu lineage. Founder of Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery.
members.bio: Jetsunma Tenzin Palmo (born 1943) is a bhikṣuṇī in the Drukpa Lineage of the Kagyu school of Tibetan Buddhism. She is an author, teacher, and founder of the Dongyu Gatsal Ling Nunnery in Himachal Pradesh, India. She is best known for having spent twelve years living in a remote cave in the Himalayas, three of those years in strict meditation retreat.

members.name: Eun-su Cho
members.card: Professor of Buddhist philosophy, Seoul National University.
members.bio: Eun-su Cho is a professor of Buddhist philosophy at Seoul National University and a longtime scholar of Korean Buddhism and Buddhist women's history. She served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2013–2021 term.

members.name: Kaytee Sumida
members.card: Lifetime member; liaison, Interreligious Council of San Diego.
members.bio: Kaytee Sumida is a lifetime member of Sakyadhita who has served on the Executive Committee across multiple terms. She has been a member of the Buddhist Temple San Diego since 1990 and serves as liaison with the Interreligious Council of San Diego.

members.name: Lien Bui
members.card: Served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee.
members.bio: Lien Bui served as Treasurer on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2013–2021 term.

members.name: Christie Yuling Chang
members.card: Taiwanese scholar and longtime Sakyadhita leader.
members.bio: Christie Yuling Chang is a Taiwanese Buddhist scholar who has served Sakyadhita in several leadership roles, including as President during the 2009–2013 term.

members.name: Paula Arai
members.card: Scholar of Japanese Buddhism and women's ritual.
members.bio: Paula Arai is a scholar of Japanese Buddhism whose work focuses on Buddhist women, ritual, and healing. She served as Vice President on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2009–2013 term.

members.name: Melissa Vincenty
members.bio: Melissa Vincenty served as Secretary on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2009–2013 term.

members.name: Maria Arroyo
members.card: Served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee.
members.bio: Maria Arroyo served as Treasurer on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2009–2013 term.

members.name: Karma Lekshe Tsomo
members.card: Co-founder of Sakyadhita; professor, University of San Diego.
members.bio: Karma Lekshe Tsomo is a professor of Buddhist Studies at the University of San Diego. She received the precepts of a novice nun in France in 1977 and full ordination in Korea in 1982. She studied Buddhism in Dharamsala for 15 years and received a doctorate in Comparative Philosophy from the University of Hawai'i in 2000. She is a founder and past president of Sakyadhita and director of Jamyang Foundation, an innovative education project for women in developing countries.

members.name: Christie Yuling Chang
members.card: Taiwanese scholar and longtime Sakyadhita leader.
members.bio: Christie Yuling Chang is a Taiwanese Buddhist scholar who has served Sakyadhita in several leadership roles, including as Vice President during the 2000–2009 term.

members.name: Carol Stevens
members.card: Served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee.
members.bio: Carol Stevens served as Secretary on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2000–2009 term.

members.name: Rebecca Paxton
members.card: Served on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee.
members.bio: Rebecca Paxton served as Treasurer on the Sakyadhita Executive Committee during the 2000–2009 term.
